title: UI and UX Design Recognition Quiz

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "User journey" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: User journey: The steps a user takes to reach a goal
wrongAnswers: Navigation: Controls that help users move through a product
Hierarchy: Arranging elements to show importance
Microcopy: Short interface text that guides users

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Wireframe" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Wireframe: A low-detail layout plan for an interface
wrongAnswers: Affordance: A cue that suggests how something can be used
Consistency: Using familiar patterns across an interface
Design system: A shared set of reusable design rules and components

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Prototype" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Prototype: An interactive or visual model used to test a design
wrongAnswers: Feedback: A system response that confirms an action or state
Error state: A design state that communicates a problem
User interface: The visible and interactive parts of a product

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Usability testing" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Usability testing: Observing users to find design problems
wrongAnswers: Accessibility: Designing products for people with varied abilities
Empty state: A design state shown when there is no content yet
User experience: The overall experience a person has using a product

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Information architecture" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Information architecture: Organizing content so people can find and understand it
wrongAnswers: Contrast: A visual difference that helps elements stand apart
Onboarding: Helping new users begin using a product
Persona: A research-based profile representing a user type
